Solum 02 — Powder Format

Solum Muscle

Sarcopenia Defense & Muscle Preservation

Product Details
Format Powder — one scoop (~11g) daily
Timing Post-workout or with breakfast
With Fat Take with a meal containing dietary fat — D3 and K2 are fat-soluble
Retail $75
Testing Independent CoA — Eurofins / ChromaDex
Why Powder 11g of active ingredients per serving cannot fit in a practical capsule format. Powder delivers the clinical dose.
The Clinical Case

Muscle is the longevity organ. Not cardiovascular fitness. Not metabolic markers. Muscle mass and strength are the single most reliable predictors of how long you will function well — not just how long you will live.

Sarcopenia — age-related muscle loss — begins in your thirties and accelerates sharply after fifty. By seventy, most people have lost 20–40% of their peak muscle mass. The consequences are functional: strength, balance, fall risk, independence, and metabolic health are all directly determined by how much muscle you preserve.

These five ingredients address sarcopenia through every mechanism at clinical doses — in a powder format because the studied amounts simply cannot fit in a practical capsule.

Ingredient Breakdown
Ingredient Dose Clinical Rationale
Creatine Monohydrate Creapure® (AlzChem, Germany) 5,000 mg The most researched ingredient in sports nutrition — hundreds of RCTs. 5g daily achieves full phosphocreatine saturation within 28 days. Supports ATP regeneration and directly counters age-related decline in muscle force. Creapure® is the only creatine with consistent independent purity verification.
Calcium HMB myHMB™ (TSI Group) — patented, single source 3,000 mg Exact dose from the Wilson et al. and Nissen et al. sarcopenia studies. HMB reduces muscle protein breakdown — it works the prevention side while Leucine works the synthesis side. Together they cover the full muscle protein turnover equation. 3g is non-negotiable.
L-Leucine — Instantized Instantized form with sunflower lecithin coating — raw L-Leucine NOT acceptable. Ensures clean dispersibility in cold water and masks bitter taste. 3,000 mg The primary mTOR-activating amino acid. Norton and Layman's leucine threshold research established that 2,500–3,000mg per serving is required to maximally trigger muscle protein synthesis. Below 2g the anabolic response is blunted. At 3g you're at the threshold that fully activates the cascade.
Vitamin D3 DSM — cholecalciferol. Microencapsulated beadlet for powder stability. 2,000 IU Vitamin D receptor expression in muscle tissue directly regulates muscle protein synthesis — a specific sarcopenia mechanism, not general wellness. Deficiency is present in ~40% of US adults and independently associated with accelerated muscle loss. Take with dietary fat for optimal absorption.
Vitamin K2 MK-7 — CWD Matrix MenaQ7® Matrix CWD (NattoPharma) — cold-water dispersible matrix form. Min 99% trans-isomer. Standard powder NOT acceptable. 100 mcg The essential cofactor for D3. Activates Matrix Gla Protein which directs calcium into bone and prevents arterial calcification. Without K2, calcium mobilized by D3 can deposit in arteries rather than bone. MK-7 has a 72-hour half-life — once-daily dosing is effective.
⚠ Required Physician Consultation

Vitamin K2 directly interacts with Warfarin and other anticoagulant medications — do not take without physician oversight if on anticoagulation therapy. Consult your physician before use if you have kidney disease (creatine) or a granulomatous condition such as sarcoidosis (Vitamin D3). Note: creatine commonly causes a temporary 1–3 lb weight increase from intramuscular water retention in the first two weeks.

Drug Interaction Reference
Vitamin K2 + Warfarin / Anticoagulants
Most critical interaction. K2 directly opposes Warfarin's mechanism. Anyone on anticoagulation therapy must not take this product without physician oversight.
Vitamin D3 + Thiazide Diuretics
Combination can elevate blood calcium. Not a concern at 2,000 IU in otherwise healthy adults, but monitor if on thiazide diuretics.
Creatine + Caffeine
Early research suggested interference; not replicated in more recent literature. No established clinical concern at standard doses.
L-Leucine + Insulin Therapy (Type 1 Diabetes)
Leucine stimulates insulin secretion. Relevant for Type 1 diabetics managing blood glucose carefully. Monitor and adjust if needed.

These statements have not been evaluated by the FDA. This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease.
Consult your physician before beginning any supplement regimen, especially if taking prescription medications.
